name: Peaches
why_now: Southern California stone fruit peaks through July. The combination of warm days and cool nights produces fruit with real sugar and real fragrance. This is the short window.
how_to_choose: Fragrant at the stem end — this is the most reliable test
Gives slightly near the top when gently pressed
buy_this_much: 4–5 peaches for one person for the week. They do not all need to be eaten at once.

flavor_paths.name: Nearly nothing
flavor_paths.description: A ripe peach over the sink. Salt if you want it.

flavor_paths.name: Mint + honey
flavor_paths.description: Sliced with fresh mint and a little honey. Breakfast or dessert.

flavor_paths.name: Savory
flavor_paths.description: Grilled or sliced alongside chicken. A little lime, a little heat.

weekday_uses: Sliced on Greek yogurt in the morning
Added to a salad with chicken or salmon
weekend_use: Grilled peaches alongside chicken thighs with corn salad.
storage: Counter until ripe. Eat within a day or two of peak ripeness. Do not refrigerate until fully ripe, and use quickly after.
one_thing_to_learn: A peach ripens from the inside out. A soft shoulder near the stem means it is ready. A hard peach left on the counter will soften but will not improve the way tree-ripened fruit does.
